verdict_reason: The single disagreement entry on adhikāraḥ meets all four criteria. (1) NAMES THE AXIS: scope of adhikāra — Shankara reads it as a contrast with jñāna-niṣṭhā (the verse applies to actors, not to the realised non-doer); Ramanuja reads it as scoped to specific Vedic-act categories (obligatory / occasional / desiderative); Prabhupada and Mukundananda flatten to 'prescribed duty.' That is the axis named precisely. (2) DOCTRINALLY SUBSTANTIVE: the explanation makes clear why this matters — Shankara's reading restricts the verse to those who are still acting (not the jñānin), Ramanuja's reading restricts it to Vedic-prescribed action categories, and the flattened reading makes it generic across both. These are real Advaita / Vishishtadvaita / popular-tradition stakes, not just translator preference. (3) TIGHT: it is a single paragraph naming Sanskrit term, contested reading, and doctrinal stake — slightly longer than one line but at the gold-standard level of specificity rather than the failing 'they differ' / 'this is contested' level. (4) NOT EDITORIALISING: the explanation does not pick a winner; it describes Prabhupada/Mukundananda as 'flattening this nuance' but characterises the flattening as 'general enough to be uncontroversial across traditions' — a description, not a verdict on which is correct. Score 9, not 10, because 'flatten' carries mild evaluative tone. PASS.
